perturbation.original_text: Buyer agrees that Buyer and its Representatives shall (i) use the Ev aluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and consummating th e Transaction (or other legal, audit or compliance purposes permitted by this Agreement);13 (ii) except as otherwise permitted by this Section 2, keep all Evaluation Material strictly confidential; and (iii) disclose Evaluation Material only to Representatives of Buyer to whom disclosure is needed to facilitate the evaluation, negotiation and/or consummation of the Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer agrees that Buyer and its Representatives shall (i) use the Ev aluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and consummating the Merger (or other legal, audit or compliance purposes permitted by this Agreement);13 (ii) except as otherwise permitted by this Section 2, keep all Evaluation Material strictly confidential; and (iii) disclose Evaluation Material only to Representatives of Buyer to whom disclosure is needed to facilitate the evaluation, negotiation and/or consummation of the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Transaction' to 'Merger' in Section 2(a) without a corresponding change in Section 1 creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement now inconsistently refers to the deal as both 'Transaction' and 'Merger'. The introduction of the term 'Merger' in section 2(a) introduces ambiguity regarding which term is the correct one when evaluating, negotiating and consummating the deal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Certain Definitions . As used in this letter agreement (this “ Agreement ”): (a) “Affiliate ” means, with respect to any Person, any other Person that is directly or indirectly Controlling, Controlled by or under common Control with such Person, where “ Control ” and derivative terms mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise. [Notwithstanding the foregoing, for purposes of Section 7, any such Person that is a portfolio company of, or an investment fund that is advised or managed by, [Buyer] [Buyer’s Parent Company] or any of its Affiliates shall not be considered an Affiliate of 1 This form was prepared in January 2015 by the Corporation Law Committee of the New York City Bar Association as a model for a unilateral confidentiality agreement ( i.e., to be used when confidential information is being disclosed by only one party). The draft attemp ts to reflect the Committee’s view of terms that may be acceptable to both a buyer and a s eller, with additional commentary provided on often-negotiated provisions. Of course, careful attention to specific fa cts and client needs is always advisable. 2 This form has been drafted assuming the target company is the disclosing party. If a selling equityholder is also a disclosing party, conforming changes should be made throughout to also reference the selling equityholder, as appropriate. 3 The disclosing party may want to insert the wo rd “negotiated” to make clear that the information may only be used in a consensual transaction (particularly if a standstill is not included). See Martin Marietta Materials, Inc. v. Vulcan Materials Co. , 56 A 3d 1072 (Del Ch. 2012) and Goodrich Capital, LLC and Windsor Sheffield & Co. Inc. v. Vector C apital Corporation (S.D.N.Y. June 26, 2012). 4 If the potential Buyer is a competitor of the Company, the parties often will establish a “clean team” or “attorneys eyes only” protocol for dealing with co mpetitively sensitive information and will enter into a “Common Interest Agreement.” Parties are encouraged to coordin ate the provisions of any Common Interest Agreement with the provisions of their confidentiality ag reement. Discontinuity between the terms of the two agreements created issues for the parties in Martin Marietta Materials. In addition, please see the footnote to Section 13 regarding an additional covenant to cons ider in the event that Buyer and the Company are competitors. 2 Buyer unless Evaluation Material or Discussions Disclosure is made available or actually provided or disclosed to such Pe rson by or on behalf of Buyer; provided that Evaluation Material or Discussions Disclosure shall not be deemed to have been made available or actually provided or disclosed to any such Person solely as a result of the fact that a Representative of the Buyer with knowledge of any Evaluation Material or Discussions Disclosure is serving on the board of directors (or similar governing body) or as an officer of such Person.] 5 (b) “Evaluation Material ” means any information or data concerning the Company or any of its Affiliates, whether in oral, visual, written, electronic or other form, that is disclosed to Buyer or any of its Representatives [before the date hereof,] 6 now or in the future by or on behalf of the Company or any of its Representatives, together with all notes, memoranda, forecasts, summaries, analyses, compilations and other writings relating thereto that are prepared by or on behalf of Buyer or any of its Representatives to the extent that they use, contain, reflect or are derived from or incorporate, in whole or in part, any such information or data. Notwithstanding the foregoing, “ Evaluation Material ” does not include any information or data that: (i) is or was independently developed by Buyer or an y of its Representatives without the benefit of any Evaluation Material;7 (ii) is or becomes generally available to the public, other than as a result of disclosure by or on beha lf of Buyer or any of its Representatives in breach of this Agreement; (iii) is or becomes available to Buyer [on a non-confidential basis] 8 from a source other than the Company or any of its Representatives, so long as that source[, to Buyer’s knowledge after reasonable inquiry,]9 is not bound by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the Company; or (iv) is already in Buyer’s posses sion at the time of disclosure by or on behalf of the Company or any of its Representatives[ as shown by Buyer’s records immediately prior to the time of disclosure] 10 from a source other than the Company or any of its Representatives, so long as that sourc e[, to Buyer’s knowledge after reasonable inquiry,]11 is not bound by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the Company. (c) “including ” means “including, without limitation.”
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Transaction' with 'Merger' in Section 2(a), while the 'Contradicted Text' (Section 1) only defines 'Transaction' as the relevant term for the agreement. The 'Perturbation Explanation' states this creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now inconsistently refers to the deal as both 'Transaction' and 'Merger,' leading to ambiguity. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction,' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer agrees that all communications by it or any of its Representatives with the ot her Party concerning the Transaction and its due diligence investigation (including requests for additional Evaluation Material, meetings with management and site visits) shall be directed solely to [___________________] , except as may otherwise be approved in advance and in writing (including by email) by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer agrees that all communications by it or any of its Representatives with the ot her Party concerning the Deal and its due diligence investigation (including requests for additional Evaluation Material, meetings with management and site visits) shall be directed solely to [___________________] , except as may otherwise be approved in advance and in writing (including by email) by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Transaction' to 'Deal' in Section 6, without providing a definition for 'Deal' and without replacing all other instances of 'Transaction' within the contract, it creates uncertainty about whether 'Deal' and 'Transaction' refer to the same thing. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement uses different terms interchangeably to refer to the same concept without clear definition.

(c) “including ” means “including, without limitation.”
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Transaction'. The changed text modifies this to 'Deal' without defining 'Deal' and without consistently updating all instances of 'Transaction' within the contract. The contradicted text includes definitions for other terms, but does not include a definition for 'Deal', and expects consistent use of defined terms. This inconsistency in terminology between 'Deal' and 'Transaction' creates ambiguity and uncertainty, directly conflicting with the need for clear and consistent definitions throughout the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer agrees that neither Buyer nor any of its Representatives will contact or communicate with any of the directors, officers, employees, customers, suppliers, distributors, licensees, licensors and clients of the Company, and other persons, including governmental agencies, having business relations with the Company, regarding the Company or the Transaction, in each case except to the extent such contacts and communications are (i) made in the ordinary course of business of Buyer or the applicable Representative s and are unrelated to the Transaction and the evaluation of the Transaction conducted by Buyer and its Representatives or (ii) approved in advance and in writing (including by email) by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer agrees that neither Buyer nor any of its Representatives will contact or communicate with any of the directors, officers, employees, customers, suppliers, distributors, licensees, licensors and clients of the Company, and other persons, including governmental agencies, having business relations with the Company, regarding the Company or the Evaluation, in each case except to the extent such contacts and communications are (i) made in the ordinary course of business of Buyer or the applicable Representative s and are unrelated to the Transaction and the evaluation of the Transaction conducted by Buyer and its Representatives or (ii) approved in advance and in writing (including by email) by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'the Transaction' to 'the Evaluation' within the first part of the first sentence introduces a contradictory reference. While the second part of the same sentence maintains 'the Transaction,' leading to an unclear understanding of which the communication restriction applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer agrees that neither Buyer nor any of its Representatives will contact or communicate with any of the directors, officers, employees, customers, suppliers, distributors, licensees, licensors and clients of the Company, and other persons, including governmental agencies, having business relations with the Company, regarding the Company or the Evaluation, in each case except to the extent such contacts and communications are (i) made in the ordinary course of business of Buyer or the applicable Representative s and are unrelated to the Transaction and the evaluation of the Transaction conducted by Buyer and its Representatives or (ii) approved in advance and in writing (including by email) by the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'the Transaction' with 'the Evaluation' in the initial restriction on communication, while the exception clauses (i and ii) still refer to 'the Transaction'. The contradicted text repeats the problematic changed text, creating a clear contradiction as to whether the communication restriction applies to 'the Transaction' or 'the Evaluation'. The change causes confusion and potentially conflicting obligations, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent that any Evaluation Material includes materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, such disclosure is inadvertent and the Company is not waiving, and shall not be deemed to have waived or diminished, its attorney work-product protections, attorney-client privileges or similar protections and privileges as a result of disclosing any Evaluation Material (including Evaluation Material related to pending or threatened litigation) to Buyer or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent that any Information includes materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, such disclosure is inadvertent and the Company is not waiving, and shall not be deemed to have waived or diminished, its attorney work-product protections, attorney-client privileges or similar protections and privileges as a result of disclosing any Evaluation Material (including Evaluation Material related to pending or threatened litigation) to Buyer or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evaluation Material' to 'Information' at the beginning of Section 10, without consistent changes throughout, creates an internal contradiction. The section starts referring to 'Information' but then switches back to 'Evaluation Material', creating ambiguity about which type of data is subject to attorney-client privilege.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To the extent that any Information includes materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, such disclosure is inadvertent and the Company is not waiving, and shall not be deemed to have waived or diminished, its attorney work-product protections, attorney-client privileges or similar protections and privileges as a result of disclosing any Evaluation Material (including Evaluation Material related to pending or threatened litigation) to Buyer or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text referred to 'Evaluation Material' in a section detailing attorney-client privilege. The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Material' with 'Information' at the beginning of the same sentence but reverts to using 'Evaluation Material' later in the same sentence and in the contradicted text. This misalignment creates ambiguity, making it unclear whether the attorney-client privilege applies to all 'Information' or only to 'Evaluation Material', thus creating an in-text contradiction and adhering to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction. The change breaks compatibility between the beginning and end of the sentence in question. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, solidifying the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, the subsequent use by such Representative of such general knowledge, skills and experience, as retained in their unaided memories, will not in itself constitute a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything in this Confidentiality Agreement to the contrary, the subsequent use by such Representative of such general knowledge, skills and experience, as retained in their unaided memories, will not in itself constitute a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly using 'Confidentiality Agreement' instead of just 'Agreement' in Section 11 creates a contradiction if some clauses in the entire Agreement are not related to the confidentiality part creating a contradiction on the scope
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Entire Agreement . This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the parties with respect to the matters set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Agreement,' suggesting the exception applies to the entire agreement. The changed text narrows the exception to the 'Confidentiality Agreement' within the broader 'Agreement.' This creates a contradiction with the 'Entire Agreement' clause, which states the agreement is the sole and entire agreement with respect to *all* matters set forth. By limiting the exception only to the 'Confidentiality Agreement,' the change implies that the 'Agreement' contains more than just confidentiality aspects, but the 'Entire Agreement' clause indicates that the 'Agreement' is entire, and covers everything, leading to a contradiction.
